title: [MEDIUM] Cap get_activity_log limits and make filtered history reliable
description: Deep sweep finding: packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/getActivityLog.ts accepts arbitrary positive limit/offset/maxPayloadChars values. Unfiltered requests can ask readLastLines for huge windows and produce oversized MCP responses. Filtered requests prefetch at most 1000 tail entries, so older matching task/epic/worker events are incorrectly invisible while hasMore can be misleading.
definitionOfDone: get_activity_log validates/clamps limit, offset, and maxPayloadChars to documented safe bounds.
Filtered queries either scan/paginate reliably enough to find older matching events or clearly return pagination metadata that does not falsely imply full history was searched.
Response-size protections apply on both MCP tool path and plugin WebSocket GET_ACTIVITY_LOG path, or the plugin path has an explicit safe cap.
Regression tests cover overlarge limit/maxPayloadChars and filtered events older than the initial tail window.
packages/moe-daemon npm test and build pass.

implementationPlan.description: Validate and clamp limit, offset, and maxPayloadChars in getActivityLogTool with explicit invalid-input error handling; document safe maximums.
implementationPlan.note: Added safe normalization for get_activity_log limit/offset/maxPayloadChars with finite-number validation, documented defaults/max values, and clamping for overlarge values. maxPayloadChars=0 remains explicit no-truncation opt-out for backward compatibility. Targeted activity-log tests passed: npm test -- --run src/tools/tools.test.ts src/state/StateManager.test.ts -t "get_activity_log|ActivityLog tool" (13 passed, 254 skipped).

implementationPlan.description: Add a filtered activity-log read path or enhanced pagination metadata so taskId/epicId/workerId filters can find older matches without falsely reporting a complete search of only the last 1000 events.
implementationPlan.note: Added readLastLinesWithMetadata and StateManager.getActivityLogWindow so activity-log queries know when older lines were omitted. get_activity_log now scans a bounded larger window for filtered queries (up to 5000 lines), returns search metadata including complete/scanned counts, and sets hasMore conservatively when older lines may contain more matches. Targeted reverseReader/activity-log tests passed: 14 passed, 259 skipped.

implementationPlan.description: Apply comparable response-size caps/truncation to plugin WebSocket GET_ACTIVITY_LOG or route it through the same helper.
implementationPlan.note: Routed plugin WebSocket GET_ACTIVITY_LOG through shared activity-log normalization/query helpers while preserving ACTIVITY_LOG payload as an array. Plugin path now uses the same default/capped limit and payload truncation behavior as the MCP tool. Targeted WebSocket/get_activity_log tests passed: 20 passed, 165 skipped.

implementationPlan.description: Add regression tests for overlarge limits, maxPayloadChars, and filtered matches older than the tail prefetch window; run daemon test/build validation.
implementationPlan.note: Added/verified regressions for clamped activity-log limits and maxPayloadChars, invalid numeric params, filtered matches older than the former 1000-line tail, and plugin WebSocket GET_ACTIVITY_LOG caps/truncation (including maxPayloadChars=0 staying bounded on WS). Red check: temporarily restored old 1000-line filtered scan and the older-match test failed; restored 5000-line scan and it passed. Validation: targeted vitest 3 files = 30 passed/254 skipped; npm run build passed; full npm test = 25 files/458 tests passed; git diff --check clean except CRLF warnings. Adversarial review: plugin path now forces bounded payloads even if caller requests maxPayloadChars=0; MCP keeps 0 as documented no-truncation opt-in for backward compatibility.

comments.author: architect-2992
comments.content: Token-budget check: keep get_activity_log bounded by default, truncate payload fields, and provide explicit maxPayloadChars/limit opt-ins for full history only when needed.

planningNotes.approachesConsidered: Preferred: clamp limits and add a filtered reverse-reader path that scans chunks until enough matches or a documented scan cap. Rejected: returning unlimited full log content.
planningNotes.codebaseInsights: MCP get_activity_log has payload truncation but no limit/maxPayloadChars cap. WebSocket GET_ACTIVITY_LOG bypasses truncation entirely. Filtered MCP queries prefetch a capped tail window before filtering.
planningNotes.risks: Full historical scans can be expensive on large logs; choose bounded streaming/reverse scanning and honest hasMore metadata. Keep malformed-line error handling fail-safe.
